```{r baseline descriptives function}
  list(var_name = "VEHTYPE", label = "Vehicle Type", type = "categorical")
)

# Initialize empty dataframe for results
baseline_descriptives <- data.frame(
  Characteristic = character(),
  Value = character(),
  Control = character(),
  Case = character(),
  P_Value = character(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)


# Process each variable
for (var in baseline_vars) {
  if (var$type == "continuous") {
    # Continuous variables
    case_mean <- mean(cases_df[[var$var_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
    case_sd <- sd(cases_df[[var$var_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
    control_mean <- mean(controls_df[[var$var_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
    control_sd <- sd(controls_df[[var$var_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
    
    p_value <- t.test(cases_df[[var$var_name]], controls_df[[var$var_name]])$p.value
    
    baseline_descriptives <- rbind(
      baseline_descriptives,
      data.frame(
        Characteristic = var$label,
        Value = "Mean (SD)",
        Control = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", control_mean, control_sd),
        Case = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", case_mean, case_sd),
        P_Value = format_p(p_value),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    )
  } else {
    # Ensure the variable is treated as a factor with all levels including NA
    current_var <- df[[var$var_name]]
    if (!is.factor(current_var)) {
      current_var <- factor(current_var)
    }
    
    # Check if there are any NAs in the full dataset
    has_na <- anyNA(current_var)
    
    # If NAs exist, add NA as a level; otherwise, keep original levels
    if (has_na) {
      current_var <- addNA(current_var)
    }
    all_levels <- levels(current_var)
      
      # Get counts and percentages with factors
      case_factor <- factor(cases_df[[var$var_name]], levels = all_levels)
      control_factor <- factor(controls_df[[var$var_name]], levels = all_levels)
      
      # Apply addNA() only if NAs exist in the full dataset
      if (has_na) {
        case_factor <- addNA(case_factor)
        control_factor <- addNA(control_factor)
      }
      
      case_counts <- table(case_factor)
      case_pct <- prop.table(case_counts) * 100
      control_counts <- table(control_factor)
      control_pct <- prop.table(control_counts) * 100
      
      # Statistical test
      cont_table <- table(df[[var$var_name]], df$HIPR)
      test_result <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(cont_table))
      if (any(test_result$expected < 5)) {
        test_result <- fisher.test(cont_table, simulate.p.value = TRUE)
      }
      
      # Add header row
      baseline_descriptives <- rbind(
        baseline_descriptives,
        data.frame(
          Characteristic = var$label,
          Value = "",
          Control = "",
          Case = "",
          P_Value = format_p(test_result$p.value),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      )
      
      # Add levels with proper handling of missing levels
      for (level in all_levels) {
        control_count <- ifelse(is.na(control_counts[level]), 0, control_counts[level])
        control_pct_val <- ifelse(is.na(control_pct[level]), 0, control_pct[level])
        case_count <- ifelse(is.na(case_counts[level]), 0, case_counts[level])
        case_pct_val <- ifelse(is.na(case_pct[level]), 0, case_pct[level])
        
        baseline_descriptives <- rbind(
          baseline_descriptives,
          data.frame(
            Characteristic = "",
            Value = level,
            Control = sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", control_count, control_pct_val),
            Case = sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", case_count, case_pct_val),
            P_Value = "",
            stringsAsFactors = FALSE
          )
        )
      }
    }
  }

# View the final table
print(baseline_descriptives)
}
```

```{r baseline descriptives listwise}
baseline_desc_fun(madymo_nhtsa_df_listwise)
baseline_desc_fun(madymo_nhtsa_df)
```



```{r multiple imputation, eval=FALSE}
# multiple imputation to take care of remaining missingness
# specify variables to not be included in imputation (1 = use in imputation)
pred <- make.predictorMatrix(madymo_nhtsa_df)
pred[, c("ID", "PSU", "PSUSTRAT", "RATWGT", "YEAR", "MAKE", "HIC15")] <- 0

# Run MICE
imputed_data <- mice(madymo_nhtsa_df, predictorMatrix = pred, m = 5)

# check distribution of observed vs. imputed data
densityplot(imputed_data, ~ BMI + ALCINV)

# save imputed_data to file
saveRDS(imputed_data, file = file.path("data", "imputed_data.rds"))

# write madymo_nhtsa_df to csv with vroom
vroom::vroom_write(madymo_nhtsa_df_imp, file = file.path("data", "madymo_nhtsa_df_mice.csv"))
```

```{r imputation objecst}
# load imputed_data
imputed_data <- readRDS(file = file.path("data", "imputed_data.rds"))

# bring imputed data back to original dataset
madymo_nhtsa_df_imp <- complete(imputed_data, action = "long", include = TRUE)

madymo_nhtsa_df_imp2 <- complete(imputed_data, "all")
```

```{r new functions for imputed multivariate survey model evaluation}
# function to print and examine ROC curve, AUC, and other metrics
roc_weighted_fun_multi <- function(design, model) {
  # Pool results (as before)
  model_pooled <- pool(model)
  
  # Extract fitted values from each imputation
  fitted_per_imputation <- lapply(model$analyses, fitted)
  
  # Average across imputations
  average_fitted <- rowMeans(do.call(cbind, fitted_per_imputation))
  
  # Extract required components from survey design
  roc_data <- data.frame(
    y = design$variables$HIPR,
    phat = average_fitted,
    weights = design$variables$RATWGT
  )

  
  # Create weighted ROC object
  mycurve <- wroc(
    response.var = "y",
    phat.var = "phat",
    weights.var = "weights",
    data = roc_data,
    tag.event = levels(roc_data$y)[2],
    tag.nonevent = levels(roc_data$y)[1],
    cutoff.method = "Youden"
  )
  
  # Calculate prevalence of the event
  event_level <- levels(roc_data$y)[2]
  prevalence <- sum(roc_data$weights[roc_data$y == event_level]) / sum(roc_data$weights)
    
  # browser()
  
  # Extract performance metrics
  metrics_list <- list(
    AUC = mycurve$wauc,
    Optimal_Cutoff = mycurve$optimal.cutoff$cutoff.value,
    Sensitivity = mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Sew,
    Specificity = mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Spw,
    # PPV
    PPV = 100 * ((mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Sew * prevalence) / 
                   ((mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Sew * prevalence) + 
                      ((1 - mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Spw) * (1 - prevalence))))
  )
  
  # Generate plot
  roc_plot <- wroc.plot(
    x = mycurve,
    print.auc = TRUE,
    print.cutoff = TRUE
  )
  
  # Return both plot and metrics
  return(list(
    mycurve = mycurve,
    metrics = metrics_list
  ))
}

# Assuming 'imputed_designs' is a list of survey designs from imputed_datasets
imputed_designs <- lapply(madymo_nhtsa_df_imp2, function(dataset) {
  svydesign(
    ids = ~ PSU,
    strata = ~ PSUSTRAT,
    weights = ~ RATWGT,
    data = dataset,
    nest = TRUE
  )
})

# function for full model performance evaluation
model_eval_imp <- function(model_list, imputed_designs = imputed_designs) {
  # Average fitted values across imputations
  fitted_per_imputation <- lapply(model_list, fitted)
  average_fitted <- rowMeans(do.call(cbind, fitted_per_imputation))
  
  # Use the FIRST design (all imputed designs are structurally identical)
  design <- imputed_designs[[1]]
  
  # Prepare ROC data
  roc_data <- data.frame(
    y = design$variables$HIPR,
    phat = average_fitted,
    weights = design$variables$RATWGT
  )
  
  # Create weighted ROC object
  mycurve <- wroc(
    response.var = "y",
    phat.var = "phat",
    weights.var = "weights",
    data = roc_data,
    tag.event = levels(roc_data$y)[2],
    tag.nonevent = levels(roc_data$y)[1],
    cutoff.method = "Youden"
  )
  
  # Calculate prevalence
  event_level <- levels(roc_data$y)[2]
  prevalence <- sum(roc_data$weights[roc_data$y == event_level]) / sum(roc_data$weights)
  
  # Extract metrics
  metrics_list <- list(
    AUC = mycurve$wauc,
    Optimal_Cutoff = mycurve$optimal.cutoff$cutoff.value,
    Sensitivity = mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Sew,
    Specificity = mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Spw,
    PPV = 100 * ((mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Sew * prevalence) / 
                   (mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Sew * prevalence + 
                      (1 - mycurve$optimal.cutoff$Spw) * (1 - prevalence)))
  )
  
  return(list(metrics = metrics_list))
}
```

```{r univariate imputed logistic regression model}
# univariate logistic regression (with multiply imputed data)
# examine correlation between DV_MPH and HIC15
fit_univariate_imp <- with(imputed_data, svyglm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_log,
  data = madymo_nhtsa_df, 
  family = quasibinomial,
  design = survey_design))
summary(pool(fit_univariate))
```

```{r multivariate imputed models}
# temporarily on hold while building pre-imputation models

# current state: starting at fit_multivariate_max_simple, uses old model building system
# function that directly follows is most recent attempt to build a function that takes in string of formula and outputs results

# maximal model to build from
# Fit multivariate model across imputations
multi_imp_model_fun <- function(formula_as_text = "HIPR ~ HIC15_log") {
  fits <- lapply(madymo_nhtsa_df_imp2, function(dataset) {
    # Rebuild survey design for THIS imputed dataset
    design_imp <- svydesign(
      ids = ~ PSU,
      strata = ~ PSUSTRAT,
      weights = ~ RATWGT,
      data = dataset,  # Explicitly use the imputed dataset
      nest = TRUE
    )
    # Fit the model
    svyglm(
      as.formula(formula_as_text),
      family = quasibinomial,
      design = design_imp
    )
  })
}

multi_results_fun <- function(formula_as_text = "HIPR ~ HIC15_log") {
  model_list <- multi_imp_model_fun(formula_as_text)
  pooled_results <- pool(model_list)
  summary(pooled_results)
}


# defaults to giving univariate model, argument can be another formula for other models
# outputs table with results
multi_results_fun()



# Convert to "mira" object (required for mice::pool)
fit_multivariate_max <- as.mira(fit_multivariate_max)
summary(pool(fit_multivariate_max))



# simple form of maximal multivariate model
fit_multivariate_max_simple <- with(imputed_data, svyglm(
    HIPR ~ HIC15_log + SEX_BIN + AGE + BMI + ALCINV + DRGINV + 
      VEHWGT + MODELYR + TIME + VEHTYPE + DV_MPH + BAGDEPLOY_BIN + 
      BELTUSE_BIN + SEATPOS,
    family = quasibinomial,
    design = survey_design
  ))
summary(pool(fit_multivariate_max_simple))


# adjust these once main model is figured out


# model including occupant variables
fit_multivariate_occ <- with(imputed_data, svyglm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
    SEX_BIN + AGE + BMI,
  data = madymo_nhtsa_df, 
  family = quasibinomial,
  design = survey_design))
summary(pool(fit_multivariate_occ))

# model including environment variables
fit_multivariate_env <- with(imputed_data, svyglm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
    ALCINV + DRGINV + VEHWGT + MODELYR + TIME + VEHTYPE,
  data = madymo_nhtsa_df, 
  family = quasibinomial,
  design = survey_design))
summary(pool(fit_multivariate_env))

# model including simulation variables
fit_multivariate_sim <- with(imputed_data, svyglm(
  HIPR ~ HIC15_log + 
    DV_MPH + BAGDEPLOY_BIN + BELTUSE_BIN + DV_MPH + SEATPOS,
  data = madymo_nhtsa_df, 
  family = quasibinomial,
  design = survey_design))
summary(pool(fit_multivariate_sim))



# print all models in ascending complexity (for showing Maurice, with simple max model)
summary(pool(fit_univariate))
summary(pool(fit_multivariate_occ))
summary(pool(fit_multivariate_env))
summary(pool(fit_multivariate_sim))
summary(pool(fit_multivariate_max_simple))
```

```{r build model_eval_fun objects}
fit_univariate_eval <- model_eval_imp(
  survey_design, 
  fit_univariate
)

fit_multivariate_occ_eval <- model_eval_imp(
  survey_design, 
  fit_multivariate_occ
)

fit_multivariate_env_eval <- model_eval_imp(
  survey_design, 
  fit_multivariate_env
)

fit_multivariate_sim_eval <- model_eval_imp(
  survey_design, 
  fit_multivariate_sim
)

fit_multivariate_max_eval <- model_eval_imp(
  survey_design, 
  fit_multivariate_max
)

fit_multivariate_max_eval_simple <- model_eval_imp(
  survey_design, 
  fit_multivariate_max_simple
)
```


```{r comparing model performance}
compared_models <- c(
  "fit_univariate", 
  "fit_multivariate_occ",
  "fit_multivariate_env",
  "fit_multivariate_sim",
  "fit_multivariate_max_simple",)

compare_df_fun(compared_models)
```


```{r make DV_MPH categories, eval=FALSE}
# create DV_MPH_two var where DVMPH is 0 if DVMPH < 35 and 1 if DVMPH >= 35
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_two <- ifelse(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH < 35, 0, 1)
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_two <- as.factor(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_two)


# create DV_MPH_three var where DVMPH is 0 if DVMPH < 25, 1 if DV_MPH is 25-45, and 2 if DV_MPH > 45
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_three <- ifelse(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH < 25, 0, ifelse(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH < 45, 1, 2))
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_three <- as.factor(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_three)


# create DV_MPH_high var where DVMPH is 0 if DVMPH < 50 and 1 if DVMPH >= 50
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_high <- ifelse(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH < 50, 0, 1)
madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_high <- as.factor(madymo_nhtsa_df$DV_MPH_high)
```



```{r visualize outcome variables}
# examine frequency of DV_MPH values for all crashes with histogram
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(x = DV_MPH)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2.5) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Crash Speeds (All)", x = "Crash Speed (mph)", y = "Frequency") +
  # x axis every 5 mph
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max_DV_MPH, by = min_DV_MPH))

# examine frequency of head injury values by speed for all crashes with histogram
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(x = DV_MPH, fill = HIPR)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Crash Speeds by Injury Status (All)", x = "Crash Speed (mph)", y = "Frequency") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = "red")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max_DV_MPH, by = min_DV_MPH))

# examine frequency of head injury values by speed for all crashes, separated by SEATPOS with histogram
ggplot(madymo_nhtsa_df, aes(x = DV_MPH, fill = HIPR)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Crash Speeds by Injury Status (All)", x = "Crash Speed (mph)", y = "Frequency") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = "red")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max_DV_MPH, by = min_DV_MPH)) +
  facet_wrap(~SEATPOS) +
  # replace fill names with "Driver" and "Passenger"
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Head injury"))

```


```{r clean environment, eval=FALSE}
# clean unnecessary objects from enviroment
# clean out all items in environment except ones in kept_items
kept_items <- c("madymo_nhtsa_df", "MADYMO_df", "explore_df", "individual_count", "vehicle_count", "data_path_2000_2015", "univariate_models", "cols_final", "cols_all")
rm(list = setdiff(ls(), kept_items))
```


```{r print dataset to csv for external examination, eval=FALSE}
library(data.table)
# print madymo_nhtsa_df to csv
fwrite(madymo_nhtsa_df, file = file.path("data", "madymo_nhtsa_df.csv"))
```

```{r DAG causal model, eval=FALSE}
# Using dagitty
library(dagitty)
dag <- dagitty("dag {
  HIC15 -> HeadInjury
  SEATPOS -> HIC15
  BELTUSE_BIN -> HIC15
  BAGDEPLOY_BIN -> HIC15
  DV_MPH_BIN -> HIC15
  Age -> HeadInjury
  Sex -> HeadInjury
  BMI -> HeadInjury
}")
plot(dag)

# Using DiagrammeR
library(DiagrammeR)
grViz("
  digraph {
    HIC15 -> HeadInjury
    SEATPOS -> HIC15
    BELTUSE_BIN -> HIC15
    BAGDEPLOY_BIN -> HIC15
    DV_MPH_BIN -> HIC15
    Age -> HeadInjury
    Sex -> HeadInjury
    BMI -> HeadInjury
  }
")
```

```{r DAG validation model, eval=FALSE}
library(DiagrammeR)

grViz("
digraph DAG {
  # Nodes (entities)
  node [shape = rectangle, style = filled, fillcolor = "lightblue"]
  HIC15 [label = "MADYMO HIC15"];
  HeadInjury [label = "Real-World Head Injury"];
  
  node [fillcolor = "lightyellow"]
  seat_position; seatbelt_use; airbag_deployment; delta_V;
  
  node [fillcolor = "lightpink"]
  Age; Sex; BMI;
  
  # Edges (relationships)
  seat_position -> HIC15
  seatbelt_use -> HIC15
  airbag_deployment -> HIC15
  delta_V -> HIC15
  
  HIC15 -> HeadInjury
  
  seat_position -> HeadInjury
  seatbelt_use -> HeadInjury
  airbag_deployment -> HeadInjury
  delta_V -> HeadInjury
  
  Age -> HeadInjury
  Sex -> HeadInjury
  BMI -> HeadInjury
  
  # Hierarchical relationships
  {rank = same; seat_position; seatbelt_use; airbag_deployment; delta_V}
  {rank = same; Age; Sex; BMI}
}
")
```
